message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Vicky's of Santa Fe (Indian Wells)
Cuisine/type signal: American / Santa Fe-influenced steakhouse with nightly live jazz
Why it surfaced in discovery: Independent Indian Wells institution since 1989; widely cited as the desert's premier live jazz/R&B dinner venue, repeatedly named OpenTable Best Live Music Venue / Best Hot Spot
Discovery sources: https://www.vickysofsantafe.com/ | https://www.vickysofsantafe.com/about | https://coachellavalleyweekly.com/vickys-of-santa-fe/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I've found search results for Vicky's of Santa Fe in Indian Wells. Here's what the reviews reveal:

## Overview
Vicky's of Santa Fe is an award-winning restaurant noted for its outstanding American Cuisine, having been consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot," "Best Live Music Venue," and "Most Vibrant Bar Scene" by OpenTable since 1989.

## Atmosphere and Entertainment
Local musicians perform nightly, with solo performers, duets, and larger groups playing jazz, R&B, Broadway tunes, and more. The venue is lively, retro Santa Fe-styled and music-forward with jazz/cabaret vibes, dancing and an energetic crowd, with guests describing a sultry, fun atmosphere with well-dressed locals and strong live music.

## Menu and Food
The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops paired with various sides, such as twice-baked potato. Reviewers recommend the blue cheese bacon wrapped dates and the meatballs and calamari.

## Mixed Reviews on Dining Quality
While many reviewers praise the entertainment and atmosphere, dining reviews are mixed. Some describe service as amazing year in and year out, with consistently delicious food and a very friendly atmosphere, while others report steaks served dry or closer to well-done than requested, with meat that lacked the texture of a great filet.

## Recommendations
The restaurant gets very busy and reservations are recommended, particularly on nights with popular bands, as arriving without a reservation may result in a wait.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vicky's of Santa Fe in Indian Wells:

Since 1989, Indian Wells' premier restaurant continues to be the place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife. The restaurant was established in 1989.

Regarding ownership, owner Marc Laliberte says, "It's not just the place. It's the people. Our guests become friends and family." Marc Laliberte is the current owner mentioned across multiple sources from recent years (2022-2026).

However, the search results do not contain detailed information about the earlier ownership history in 1989 or how ownership may have changed over the years. The results primarily focus on current operations under Marc Laliberte's ownership and the restaurant's general background.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Vicky's Of Santa Fe - Key Information

**Review Metrics:**
- Total Reviews: 293
- Average Rating: 3.8 of 5 bubbles
- Ranking: #5 of 25 restaurants in Indian Wells

**Recent Review Themes:**
Recent feedback highlights mixed experiences. Positive comments praise the "bone-in ribeye," live music entertainment, and ambiance. Critics mention inconsistent service quality, with complaints about wait times exceeding reservations and "cold" baked potatoes. Several reviewers note staffing issues and management responsiveness concerns.

**Awards & Recognition:**
The restaurant has earned "Best Hot Spot," "Best Live Music Venue," and "Most Vibrant Bar Scene" designations from OpenTable. It also holds a Travelers' Choice award, reserved for establishments "consistently earning great reviews" and ranked within the top 10% on Tripadvisor.

**Notable Details:**
Operating since 1989, the venue features prime steaks, fresh seafood, and a walk-in wine cellar. Live music performances occur nightly at no cover charge, with two sets on weekends. Hours: 4:30 PM-10:00 PM daily (11:00 PM Friday-Saturday).

Based on the search results, here are some of the best live music dinner options in Palm Desert and the Coachella Valley:

## Top Restaurants with Live Music & Jazz

**Larkspur Grill** - Located just off El Paseo in Palm Desert, Larkspur Grill offers fresh flavors, handcrafted cocktails, and live music Wednesday-Saturday, featuring jazz to cover bands.

**Trio, Eight4Nine Restaurant & Lounge, and El Mirasol** - These establishments are known for their culinary delights accompanied by the smooth sounds of jazz.

**The Purple Room** - This retro supper club hosts Rat Pack–style tributes during the week, and nationally known jazz and cabaret artists on weekends.

**The Nest** - An elegant restaurant featuring resident musicians including Kevin Henry (Sundays and Mondays), Marisa Corvo (Tuesdays), and Bryan Magsayo (Wednesdays through Saturdays).

**Wildest Restaurant + Bar** - This Palm Desert spot showcases the creativity of the local music scene, featuring original artists with distinctive styles ranging from electro-pop to soul and indie rock.

**Eddie V's Prime Seafood** - A luxury dining destination known for premium seafood and steaks, where guests can enjoy live music trios in the V Lounge.

**Adobe Grill** - Features live jazz trio Friday nights and live mariachi music Saturday through Thursday.

These restaurants offer a variety of atmospheres, from upscale dining experiences to more casual settings, all combined with quality live entertainment.

Based on my search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Indian Wells for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**Nobu Indian Wells**
Nobu Indian Wells brought a new level of fine dining to the area with its global reputation for innovative Japanese-Peruvian fusion cuisine. The restaurant is known for dishes like black cod miso, yellowtail sashimi with jalapeño, and rock shrimp tempura, catering to discerning diners seeking a five-star experience.

**The Pink Cabana**
Tucked inside the Sands Hotel & Spa, The Pink Cabana presents a chic, Moroccan-inspired atmosphere and a Mediterranean menu, offering dishes like Moroccan lamb chops, house-made pastas, and a delightful mezze platter.

**Vue Grille & Bar**
Located at the Indian Wells Golf Resort, Vue Grille & Bar offers a refined yet welcoming environment with floor-to-ceiling windows and panoramic views of the surrounding Santa Rosa Mountains. The menu features contemporary American cuisine with fresh, local ingredients, including standout dishes such as prime ribeye, miso-glazed Chilean sea bass, and short rib ravioli.

**The Nest**
The Nest is Indian Wells' most famous restaurant and lounge, serving since 1965 with delicious food, wonderful atmosphere, great entertainment, and excellent service.

**Frank's Place Restaurant & Lounge**
Located at the Indian Wells Resort Hotel, Frank's Place specializes in fine American-Continental cuisine prepared with the freshest ingredients and features an exceptional wine cellar.

As of March 2, 2026, customer recommendations on OpenTable highlight Vicky's of Santa Fe, Kestrel A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge, and Nobu Indian Wells.

Based on the search results, Vicky's of Santa Fe in Indian Wells, California has been consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot," "Best Live Music Venue," and "Most Vibrant Bar Scene" by OpenTable. The restaurant is an award-winning establishment noted for its outstanding American cuisine and has been operating since 1989. Entertainment abounds in the Lounge Room with a live music program overseen by desert legend John Stanley King, with electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Vicky's of Santa Fe: Key Information

**History & Longevity:**
The restaurant had been operating for 27 years at the time of this May 2016 article, placing its opening around 1989. It's described as "a desert gem" with established credibility in the region.

**Location:**
Indian Wells, situated just south of Highway 111

**Ownership:**
No ownership details are provided in the article.

**Awards:**
None mentioned.

**Recent Updates:**
"The restaurant was remodeled two years ago to the neutral earth tones that are exhibited today," indicating renovations around 2014.

**Music & Entertainment:**
The venue features live performances seven nights weekly, spanning multiple genres. Featured artists include Mike Costley & Trio (Tuesday), Lizann Warner (Wednesday), Carolyn Martinez & Trio (Thursday), and John Stanley King Live! with "A Band of Four" (Friday evening). Programming encompasses "Jazz, Ballad, Swing, Rhythm & Blues" and various other styles.

**Credentials & Atmosphere:**
Described as featuring "lively elegance" with a ballroom, bar, and dining areas. The establishment maintains "a walk-in 4,000 bottle wine cellar with an extensive collection of wines."

**Notable Attributes:**
Known for generous portion sizes and fresh, handmade desserts prepared daily on-site.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurant recommendations in Indian Wells and Rancho Mirage:

## In Rancho Mirage

**Double Date** - A new dining experience at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa inspired by the Coachella Valley's reputation as the 'date capital of the world'. Everything is made in-house, including the sauces and pastas, with a sophisticated menu that elevates California produce with desert-inspired touches.

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Perched cliffside atop Coachella Valley, this elegant steak house at The Ritz-Carlton serves only the finest cuts of American Wagyu, all dry-aged in house.

**Shame on the Moon** - A fine-dining date night hot spot that has been operating since 1984, featuring intimate, ornate and elegant dual dining rooms.

**The Penney** - A cosmopolitan bistro with sophisticated cocktails and retro 1950s style, featuring palm tree motifs and stunning indoor-outdoor patio spaces.

## In Indian Wells

**Cava** - A Californian restaurant combining tropical elegance with innovative dining and valley views while dining poolside on the outdoor terrace, perfect for a romantic dinner for two.

**Kestrel** - A Richard Blais Kitchen featuring American classics and coastal catches in a fresh, California-modern space, perched atop Indian Wells Golf Resort with views of mountain peaks and ombre sunsets.

**Vicky's of Santa Fe** - An award-winning restaurant noted for outstanding American cuisine, consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot," "Best Live Music Venue" and "Best Bar Scene".

Based on the search results for vickysofsantafe.com, here's what I found:

**About the Restaurant:**
Vicky's Santa Fe is located in Indian Wells, CA and offers authentic Santa Fe dining with bold flavors, steaks, seafood, and pasta.

**Menu:**
The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops with various sides, including a classic twice-baked potato. The restaurant has a menu page showcasing their offerings with photos and reviews.

**Reservations:**
Dinner reservations are needed daily from 5 p.m. to 9:30 p.m. in all Dining Rooms and the Music Lounge. Reservations for dinner are recommended and accepted in the dining rooms only.

**Additional Features:**
The restaurant also features a walk-in wine cellar with California wines from well-known winemakers to select boutique vintners, and entertainment in the Lounge Room with a live music program featuring electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week.

content.text: I have enough verified data to complete the assessment.

input.name: Vicky's of Santa Fe
input.corridor: Indian Wells (Highway 111 / Indian Wells resort corridor, between Palm Desert El Paseo and La Quinta)
input.cuisine_or_type: American steakhouse with Santa Fe-influenced decor; prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb; live jazz/R&B nightly with no cover charge

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 457 reviews; Tripadvisor 293 reviews; Google approx. 571 reviews per third-party aggregator [UNVERIFIED — Google count not directly confirmed]
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive on atmosphere/music; mixed on food execution and service consistency
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025-2026) reviews consistently praise the live music programming, energy, and bar scene; food and service reviews are mixed — positives cite bone-in ribeye, dates, calamari; negatives cite overcooked steaks, cold sides, wait times exceeding reservations, and management responsiveness
reputation.longevity: Operating since 1989 (~37 years); current owner identified in press as Marc Laliberte (2022-2026 sources). Earlier ownership chain [UNVERIFIED]
credentials: OpenTable Diners' Choice recognitions cited as 'Best Hot Spot', 'Best Live Music Venue', 'Most Vibrant Bar Scene' (frequency/exact years [UNVERIFIED])
Tripadvisor #5 of 25 restaurants in Indian Wells
Featured in Coachella Valley Weekly (2016) and Coast to Coast Newspaper (2022)

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'date night Indian Wells', 'best restaurants Indian Wells', and live-music dining queries in Coachella Valley. Notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life's 'Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley' roundup despite being repeatedly OpenTable-recognized for live music — a meaningful gap. Does not surface for El Paseo / Palm Desert fine dining queries (correctly, since it's Indian Wells).
agent_visibility.appears_in_queries_detail: Wins: 'live music dinner Indian Wells', 'jazz dinner Coachella Valley' (partial), OpenTable Indian Wells lists. Loses: regional 'best jazz clubs Palm Desert' lists tend to surface The Purple Room, Melvyn's, The Nest, Larkspur, Adobe Grill ahead of or instead of Vicky's.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are generally accurate (American/steak/seafood, live music nightly since 1989). Some sources mislabel city as 'Palm Desert' (Chamber of Commerce, Restaurant Guru) — the actual address is 45100 Club Dr, Indian Wells — a small but real NAP-consistency issue agents could propagate.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Own site (vickysofsantafe.com) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetchers (bot protection / WAF), which itself is an agent-visibility negative — LLM crawlers and retrieval agents cannot read the canonical site. Site has clean human-readable IA (about, menus, reservations, live-music, show-supper-club, reviews pages) but schema.org markup presence could not be verified due to the 403. Reservations route through OpenTable (good third-party schema coverage).

competitors: The Nest (Indian Wells — direct live-music dinner competitor since 1965)
Kestrel: A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge (Indian Wells Golf Resort)
Vue Grille & Bar (Indian Wells Golf Resort)
The Purple Room (Palm Springs — competing for the live-jazz-supper-club dining dollar)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — long-running independent anchor for the Indian Wells dining/nightlife scene, one of the few non-resort-owned destination restaurants in the city
query_wins: Indian Wells date night with entertainment
OpenTable best live music venue Coachella Valley

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent (owner Marc Laliberte per press; not a chain, not resort-owned, not hotel-attached — freestanding at 45100 Club Dr)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded website with multiple content pages (about, menus, live-music calendar, show-supper-club, NYE landing page, reviews page); active OpenTable presence with paid placement features; consistent local press cycle (Coachella Valley Weekly, Coast to Coast, Palm Springs Life directory); booking-platform partnerships. No visible agency footprint, no Palm Springs Life editorial feature in recent jazz roundup — suggesting marketing is owner-driven and reservation-platform-dependent rather than PR-driven.
convertibility.reasoning: Independently owned by a named, reachable operator (Marc Laliberte) at a single freestanding location with a 37-year operating history. Clear evidence of marketing spend (own domain with deep content, OpenTable featured presence, paid directory listings, annual NYE marketing pages) but the spend pattern is heavy on transactional platforms (OpenTable) and light on editorial/SEO/agent-readable infrastructure. The 403 to automated fetchers on the own site, the absence from Palm Springs Life's flagship live-music feature despite category dominance, and the city-mislabeling across third-party directories all indicate concrete, addressable visibility problems that an AICV-style intervention could improve. High convertibility.

Third-party directories (Chamber of Commerce, Restaurant Guru) mislabel city as Palm Desert; actual address is Indian Wells — NAP consistency issue
